query_normalized: evaluating the impact of code style on llm code generation diversity computer science
rationale: The paper addresses "diversity" in the context of wireless communication signal processing (specifically bit-interleaved coded multiple beamforming), which is a distinct physical construct from "code generation diversity" in Large Language Models. This is a case of distinct constructs sharing only homonym keywords ("diversity" and "code"), failing to meet any inclusion criteria for the user's computer science research question.
